title: skopeo copy --decryption-key parameter cannot be resolved

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out.

Can you share the exact error you receive.  Running your example throws the following error:

> FATA[0000] Invalid decryption keys: open --provider: no such file or director

There is no `--provider` flag in skopeo and both `--{de,en}cryption-key` expect a string input (i.e., path to the key file).

@vrothberg  I'm sorry I wrote the above command wrong. The correct command should be: 
```
OCICRYPT_KEYPROVIDER_CONFIG=/etc/containerd/ocicrypt/ocicrypt_keyprovider.conf skopeo copy --encryption-key provider:enc oci:example oci:example-enc
```
And:
```
OCICRYPT_KEYPROVIDER_CONFIG=/etc/containerd/ocicrypt/ocicrypt_keyprovider.conf skopeo copy --decryption-key provider:dec oci:example-enc oci:example
```

I'm out for the week. Let me take a look when I'm back next week.

There was a similar bug with an older version that was fixed I believe.
Could be related.


On Tue, Oct 19, 2021, 12:36 AM Valentin Rothberg ***@***.***>
wrote:

> @lumjjb <https://github.com/lumjjb> can you have a look?
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you were mentioned.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/skopeo/issues/1479#issuecomment-946444108>,
> or unsubscribe
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AAXLDBX3HK3K4EGIKYG346TUHUNYJANCNFSM5FSYZJMQ>

>
>
Starting to look at this, tagging @pravinrajr9 who worked on this feature

I don't believe we use the `--provider` flag, that is referring to something else not related to encryption, all that is needed is to add provider as a prefix to the encryption keys, like 

```    
$ OCICRYPT_KEYPROVIDER_CONFIG=/home/vagrant/ocicrypt.conf bin/skopeo copy --encryption-key provider:simplecrypt:abc  oci:alpine oci:encrypted
```

can you try some of these examples:

https://github.com/containers/ocicrypt/blob/main/docs/keyprovider.md
I Just tried with example given over here https://github.com/containers/ocicrypt/blob/main/docs/keyprovider.md. It works

> $ OCICRYPT_KEYPROVIDER_CONFIG=/home/vagrant/ocicrypt.conf bin/skopeo copy --encryption-key provider:simplecrypt:abc  oci:alpine oci:encrypted

@jialez0 can you retry by providing the provider as suggested by @lumjjb?

@lumjjb Yes, I know the example in the documentation works, but my problem is that when the keyprovider used for encryption and the keyprovider used for decryption are not the same (of course, they will use the same key), there will be problems.

Ah i see what you mean, so the way the name of the provider is used is through the creation of annotations, so if you inspect the encrypted image, you will see the annotation for the layer have something like `org.opencontainers.....provider.enc`, the name is used to represent a protocol.

In this case it looks like you are trying to create a threat model separation of encryption and decryption, in this case, an approach would be to use different `OCICRYPT_KEYPROVIDER_CONFIG` for the encryption and decryption. This can also be done by creating one server and having the key only accessible to the runtime where decryption happens.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I believe the issue has been addressed, waiting on author to try the solution out.
Thanks everybody!  I am going to close.  Feel free to reopen if needed.
